text:
This paper deals with the philosophy of education carried out by KH. M.A. Sahal Mahfudh, an important Islamic educationalist of today’s Indonesia. Applying qualitative library research approach, the author analyzed several important works of Kiai Sahal, as well as a number of other secondary references. The author explains that the Islamic educational ideology proposed by Kiai Sahal can be classified into three categories, namely integrated, transformative, and contextual. This concept is clearly shows how he intends to abolish the existing dividing walls between the Islamic and secular sciences, and to make education carry out by an Islamic school as having spirit of completeness in accordance with NDSA (Basic Values of Sholih Akrom).

The study found out that Kiai Sahal’s views are a valuable reference for modernizing the Islamic education in Indonesia which can relate with curriculum reform and also the connection between spiritual and academic development. Sahal’s ideas has started to develop a strong impact in some of the contemporary Indonesian educational policies and practices throughout the country’s vast terrain even though there are many challenges and obstacles that need to be over come such as the presence of resistant actor’s from schools, madrasah and also constraints of the resources. This study consequently is anticipated to strengthen the ongoing critical discourse in Indonesia on the establishment of a modern Islamic educational system which at the same time remains connected with the traditional Islamic values and also is capable of dealing with the complexities of modern knowledge that is needed for the Indonesian children to compete and survive in the global era.
